reviews.review_description:
We bought this for our son during the Christmas sale two years ago and noticed and improved swing and overall stats for his game. We liked it so much that bought another year subscription for him. 

The AI swing analysis and feedback it is a big plus. We also like the ability for multiplayers to have some of his friends join him at home to train. Definitely recommend it to everyone who wants to improve their swing skills.

reviews.review_title: i just wanna play baseball bro
reviews.review_description: this sh is so expensive like it looks fun but it costs to much and  just got rid of our membership or whatever and robbed my parent of like100$ just go to IRL cages or something

I purchased this for my son. He is 13 and just started playing baseball. His first comment was…”I just saw/hit more pitches in the past 30 min than I do all week at practice!” Facts on that one! This is great to take anywhere!

reviews.review_title: It is nice but there is one thing.
reviews.review_description: I really enjoy the many many options it gives you as to what you want to face. I do think the pitches given are realistic and can no doubt help you for real life baseball. However, I feel like the tracking of my swing is not all the way accurate, my swing velocity is way slower then it was in real life, the bat does sometimes not go where you’d like but overall, I enjoy this product a lot.

I purchased WIN for my son as his travel team coach was a firm beliver and had a teams meeting with the team and WIN rep and I have to admit I wasn't sold on the product but I got it and my son loved it so we got a competition out of it. We got the attachment for his actual bat but if you have a small space the WIN bat maybe a option for you. I do like the fact of it provides the data and it really allows them to see different pitches which is awesome. I would recommend this as an assistant but you still have to get live reps and go to the batting cages as the game day result will show up. Why are you here still reading this review, go subscribe for this great game changer.

reviews.review_title: Greatest way to get reps on a rainy day
reviews.review_description: Have been using this to tune up my skills and have been loving the swing feedback and the ability to get batting reps anytime anywhere (space allowing). Just wish it had haptic feedback but other than that fantastic training tool.

I got this for my son because we live in Northern PA and our winters offer no opportunity to practice during the 6 months of snow we get. AFTER a small learning curve he loves it and asks to practice constantly. Love your product
I can’t even play the game, I need a member ship, which is like 20 or so bucks per month, could be less, but really frustrating since it could be a good game.

Bat hand calibration is stuck on the wrong controller. The switch bat hand trigger prompt does not respond despite repeated attempts, restarts, and recalibration.

Waited a week for the bat attachment to come and it switched hands on me mid session and I haven’t been able to get it to work at all.
It has some flaws here and there but it's still in development and still improving and it's genuinely made me a better hitter in all categories.
The game is so fun and it is very accurate. Also I love that it is partners with east coast Sox. ￼
Helped me at lot with my swing and did better in game because of it.

reviews.review_title: The short bat is a game changer
reviews.review_description: I love the game so much love the bats I can do every level of baseball I got the short bat yesterday I bet it can hit home runs first time the controller fell out second time in worked
The best app i have used

Purchased this for my son to get better with some training at home, it’s very glitchy and when we put the attachment on the bat seems to hover in mid air and can’t be used. Overall, not worth the money I spent. ￼
The game is great theirs is hundreds of pitches and good settings for different bats, but the subscription is little to much money because $35 each month.
Great for timing and building confidence which is what it’s all about. My son his been consistently hitting since using WIN! Best investment I’ve ever made in any training equipment!!!
I’ve been injured for a while now but Win Reality has helped me stay active and see a ton of pitches although I can’t step in a real batters box yet.
So here’s the thing why is the game free but you have to pay a subscription? That’s not a good way to make a game popular
Have watched my son play the game many times and it looks great and really helps to get practice in during the rainy days, nights, etc.. I was hesitant because of the cost, but worth every penny!!
This is such a great tool. Love being able to use this for more game reps
I like the game because they offer different types of ball speeds that I can learn from in my timing swing other than that its fun and great ..just the price is a lil much thow but it's great game
Itvis super weird and it won't register the bat Evan my dad can't understand it
Cost money. overpriced as heck. 5 stars if free
You get it it’s free yeah but you get 10 free balls then it’s a $35 subscription per month I think it’s fun but $35 is to much

This has helped my son see the ball better with his at bats. It has increased his confidence, and has helped him with his swing timing. A great tool to use when you are at home and can’t face a live pitcher. Highly recommended for anyone looking to better themselves.
So when I go in to the game I can’t choose a bat and I still can’t do it when I load it in again

My son has been using this for a few weeks now and it seems to be helping him with his pitch tracking.  I wish there was a setting to use third party handles for the controller but as of this time there isnt.  My sons only complaint (because we dont have the bat attachment) is that the weight is not there and hand tracking between the meta quest 3s and real life lines him up a bit lower than in actual.  I havent had a look Into the settings to see if I can adjust it.

I also dont like how the first screen when you pop up is an ad to buy the premium version.
Helped my son improve his swing alot.A great game the developers have made.
This is the absolute best VR baseball game on the meta quest. 10/10 recommend.
It dosent want to load the Home Screen
Supa Heat Fire. Best game on the market

Trying out this app for extra softball practice during the winter. Put the bat attachment on a -10 fast pitch bat to help offset the weight of the Meta controller and get the bat weight closer to a slow pitch bat. Took a session or two to get used to the tracking, would like if there was more bat speed data in the hitting summary. Play mode is fun to simulate those clutch at bat situations. Live pitch is fun with family. Still gotta figure out how to do the swing analysis. Could not figure out how to reposition home plate right away, took a Google search, wasn’t clear in the app. Otherwise the apps are fairly easy to use.
Why do I have to pay a subscription to play a GAME

I got this for my 10yo son who plays travel ball. 

First should this is not replacement for batting practice, this helps with tracking, timing and identifying types of pitches. You can go from very slow 38mph (If I remember correctly) pitches to college level pitching, left and right hand pitchers with all the different types of pitches. My son enjoys it alot and basically looks at it as a game but it has helped him with his timing a good bit. We have the short bat and the bat does disappear on you and there is some lag but not to the point that is horrible.

The swing analysis is actually pretty legit. We use it a good bit and the scoring feature is something I really like and my son (being a boy) looks at it as a game wanting to get a high score. 

Pricing.... its pretty expensive for a subscription platform, honestly will your child use it enough to pay that much monthly? Most likely not after the newness wears off, but it is a good short term subscription to keep the rust off during winter or to help work on a few mechanics tweaks.

To the company... consider this.... lets face it lower costs equal more people subscribing and the profits climb, higher cost equal less people and less profit. 

Make it more affordable and you will get more profits and more kids can benefit from this.

It's a video game and no video game is worth this much money. Imagine a subscription for a table tennis game. Same thing. You get reps in if you're working on something mechanically specific but the results are no where near simulation which means contact and consistency in the video game that doesn't match real life. 

I play Competitive Adult League Baseball

reviews.review_title: If I could rate 199 stars I would
reviews.review_description: Perfect for just having fun and getting better love it

We got the VR and WinReality as an early Christmas present for my 11yr old to get some extra swings in over the winter (and hopefully longer due to the cost investment). 

I really like the concept. You can select the types of pitches you want and the speed at which they are coming at you. They also have pitch recognition training which is nice to add on to the hitting. I would have loved to also add the SwingAI but it was already getting costly enough. 

We opted for the short bat instead of the bat attachment. It looked to cumbersome for my son to attach it and take it off each time. Sadly, I did not see the fine print that said the short bat is recommended for 55mph and below. Well, I probably will not get much use out of that investment and will also now have to purchase the bat attachment. 

The one frustrating part is the bat tends to “disappear” in between pitches quite a bit. It could be user error but it really takes away from the training and enjoyment. My son has to tap the bat or the ground with it before he can select the next pitch. 

I do like the casting feature. I can watch his swings on my phone (have not tried to cast to a TV). Though as his head moves so does the picture so there is a lot of shaking going on. 

All in all, it has been an ok investment. I am not sure the cost will justify it BUT we just started using it so I hope I am proved wrong.

This is a great training tool, with plenty of different options and variations to keep it fun and engaging. Great realism compared to other games/worlds ive been in on Meta. That being said, I have some suggestions and possible tweaks to make it better. I would allow the plate to be moved within the training sequences, and make bat hand/length/orientation easier to do, both should be floating options available throughout the trainings at anytime. Also, ive got the short bat and the bat (in game) glitches all over the place, and you cannot make ANY bat adjustments with the controller attached to the short bat, I regret that purchase as of now, hopefully it gets better with more reviews about it.

(played with bat attachment)
This app is great when it boils down to timings and pitch recognition. I see the potential and extensive benefits in being able to see all sorts of pitches from home. However, the physical replication of bat physics is a significant drawback. Tracking often feels lackluster (even when following all optimization guidelines) and the swing physics lack realism.

Playing becomes frustrating at times and certain "drills" integrated into the program (such as the open 45 drills) don't make practical sense in a VR setting. WIN should stop including drills advertised as 'real-life' equivalents when they lack the physical accuracy to be effective, as practicing them in this environment can be harmful to a player's actual swing. Without realistic barrel physics or physical haptic feedback, there is a genuine risk to a player's mechanics; the system tends to reward contact and raw bat speed over proper form. 

All in all, I believe that WIN is a great tool for timing, pitch recognition, and just getting reps in general. However, its potential disruption to a player's hitting form makes it harmful and unrealistic for overall development if treated as a substitute for real-life batting practice. WIN is a great ADDITION to a player's training program, having most of its potential extracted when working in tandem with real-life training.

The Win Reality app has proven to be a great resource and training tool for my boys.  The different lessons and drills help to keep them engaged and having fun.  I was very impressed with the Swing AI functionality as it really allowed us to break down the swing and focus on form.  I am excited to see how it translates to the field once the season starts.  I think it really helps build overall confidence and will make then more comfortable at the plate.

reviews.review_title: Love this for working on timing
reviews.review_description: WIN Reality Softball works great with working on timing for hitting.   Having a problem hitting drop balls?  Set it up with a pitcher with the speed you see on the weekend and have your athlete change their approach to hit any pitch.   We worked on moving to the front of box and hitting whatever speed we set it to.
It's good but there's a lot of glitches and stuff
It pretty good but I think the ev and distance is wrong. Cause my pr irl is 85 but in the game it is only 70

Stepped into the WIN world for the first time, and pretty cool being inside a stadium. The hits and speeds seem accurate as long as you calibrate the length well enough. We have the attachment that goes on the bat because I wanted to have the actual weight. My son is in 10/11u select ball, so this has been a great way to get the swings in when there is no practice. Only big issue is the bat keeps dissapearing and you probably look like a fool shaking it all over trying to get it to re-appear. Not sure if that is the WIN software or Meta's issue (I know, first-world problems). But with the cost of the subscription, you would expect that to work better. Other than that, it's a great training tool, and at least it's a good swing workout. But nothing beats the real thing!
glitches and the bat disappears   All the time
im sorry but no they need a free play theres no i wasted 1 hour and 46 min trying to find out and when i did its telling me to pay no way

reviews.review_title: Excellent training for any  age hitter !
reviews.review_description: My son got win reality for Christmas and loves it.  Has improved his pitch recognition and timing.  He was late on pitches during the season and has been working  to improve it. The training plans are great  and keeps him structured in his work out.  I highly recommend win reality to any player looking to Iimprove at the plate this year
no one paying that much for a game that is crazy lower it dude that's way to high for the money count

This has been a great resource for my boys and I. It’s really for pitch identification and to understand your zone. It will not help your swing so keep that in mind. Overall there are a lot of analytics that are really cool and helpful. Also a great winter activity as well can’t get out onto the field in the winter time here.
We got this app for me to practice my swing and it helped me SOOOOOOO MUCH. We saw a big improvement in my swing and I just can't thank them enough

This app and training system is awesome! The training and skill development is LEGIT! We are using the bat attachment for baseball and softball, and it’s been a breeze! We like to cast to the tv so we can all watch and help coach, as our players are practicing. It’s been a great training tool and we are very happy with our addition!

reviews.review_title: Great way to get swings in
reviews.review_description: This is a great way to get some swings in during the off-season. It does a good job of providing feedback and giving you different looks. Some of the newer features have made progress in helping to expand the information that you receive. I'm optimistic that it will continue to develop as a tool to keep making adjustments before the season starts so you can take it to the practice field and ultimately to the plate during the game.

reviews.review_title: One of the best hitting tools available!
reviews.review_description: My son was struggling with making contact with the pitching machine.  When he started using Win VR for a week, my son was able to be on time with the pitching machine and making contact. And not just contact, solid hits right back at the machine. He got his confidence back from using Win VR.

I got the WIN Reality game and a year-long subscription to help my son work on his batting. It can sometimes be a little glitchy, but I expected it couldn't be perfect. It still does what it says it does. He is able to practice his batting inside when he can't go outside to do it. I can already see improvements, and he has been at it for two weeks. I really like the swing AI on the app because it shows what he was doing incorrectly and provides drills to fix it. There is so much content to watch on the app and the VR app that we haven't been able to go through everything just yet. From what I've seen, I'm impressed. When you set it up, you set it up for the age of the person using it. I would recommend this if your kid struggles with hitting the ball on time and judging distance.

reviews.review_title: Win Reality is an amazing training tool!
reviews.review_description:
This is by far the best training tool for the winter/off-season months. It’s such a great way to perfect your timing and swing decisions. My son (who is 7) and I both use this. For him, it’s a great way to perfect his batting skills while also having a blast doing it. I would highly recommend this to anyone who wants to improve their batting skills, regardless of age. 

Pros:
Improving swing timing.
Improving swing decision.
Learning to recognize pitch type based off release point.
Very realistic swing data projections. 
Improves skills while having fun doing it. 


Cons:
It would be nice to have different stadiums to choose from for the kids. 
Having a coach pitch option from 30 feet would be great to engage more young kids. 
Having actual MLB pitchers and their specific pitches would be really cool.
Every time I swing I have to wait 2 minutes for the bat to get to the right spot. Just play real baseball

reviews.review_title: A bit glitchy, but a good tool
reviews.review_description: Purchased the subscription along with a Meta Quest 3S headset for my son (9yo) for Christmas.  The short bat can be a bit glitchy and positioning the plate is tricky. We do have to keep resetting to be able to line the bat up from pitch to pitch.  But overall my son has been able to take healthy cuts from the comfort of our home with 6 inches of snow on the ground.  I wouldn't be able to say that if not for this device and tech.

Got this for my 11 year old for Christmas and she has been loving it, especially the coaching that comes with it. She has done all of the exercises it told her to do to get better. Even her coach has done it and said it’ll really help her. Well worth it. Still just trying to figure out the app piece

My boys got this for Christmas and the whole family absolutely loves it! My son plays travel baseball and needs to get in extra batting practice. It’s been such an easy and fun way to practice! The whole family has tried it and competed against each other for fun. It’s really helped my ball player practice, try to fix his swing, and get in more reps.

My son received a subscription to this tool for Christmas and he absolutely loves it. As others have pointed out, the game does glitch from time to time with the bat, but he can quickly recalibrate and continue playing. Having the ability to get “live” at bats during the off season is key to continuing to grow and improve at this sport.

We bought for our son to use for winter hitting and training and it’s been fun playing with him and sharing to the TV to watch!  Only negative is the delay on the TV and setting up multiple profiles not user friendly in the game!  Highly recommend!

So I actually just bought this for my son. His main sport is ice hockey but because he's doing that so often I wanted something he could do at home and still get some reps in. He actually really likes it and he said it feels really realistic. Especially in exhibition mode where you are in situational mode. Basically let's say you're down 1 and it's the 9th inning and you're up. We also did get the small bat but haven't used it yet but I'm really excited for that because it will make the swing more realistic and because it's also weighted so it's even more realistic.
I think I'm going to try it out myself as well. It is a little spendy but if you plan to use this multiple times a week it will pay off no problem. Very good product and I highly recommend it.
This is a great tool for someone who needs more reps during the cold winter. Highly recomend for people that dont have many practices or for people that live in cold weather area's.

Love this ot great to gain confidence at the plate for players who don’t have much of it this is a great tool to step in the box and take game like reps i take about 100 swings evrey night and it keeps me confident at the plate

Christmas gift for my son who is serous about baseball.  First time with 3s goggles as well - only have them because of Win.  It’s fun, however very glitchy, the bat keeps sticking/freezing and the perimeter keeps moving in on us, annoyingly showing the grid as we get pitched to, and the bat will freeze in the air somewhere right when you need it… We’re still in assessment mode this, is it helpful or not??, we have a rapsodo / pitch machine so we know what his swing actually does and there are some results on this thing that just don’t seem right…. Still going to play around with it but so far it’s been frustratingly fun
This is a great tool for anyone practicing baseball. Sometimes the tracking is a bit off but it works the same.
Too much money
We love our VR with the WIN app.  We play baseball and it is so life like! We added the bat attachment and love it!

My daughter received the WIN subscription for Christmas. She absolutely loves the different programs within the VR app. She can practice, and play with ease. She’s even started calling the pitches before swinging. It’s a fun way to practice and get in those reps.

Got this app for my son and daughter for Christmas. They are spending more time training on the VR than actually in the cage. My son had a game last week and went 6 for 6. It was great to see him hit the ball with confidence!

They won’t even tell you how much it costs until you sign up. It’s 30 bucks a month or 250 a year to play a VR game ha ha. I guess it’s not preying on kids if their parents pay for it right?

Drop 20 or 30 bucks on a ping pong game and get good at that instead. Give your parents a break.

reviews.review_title: This app is really good for developing
reviews.review_description: I firstly got the app and went into the exhibition mode and I got really hooked. This app also has a bunch of weekly workouts to try and even a really well in app batting practice mode with tons of great timing exercises that’s a must try. I love this app!
I love win reality, It has helped me improve my swing and build my confidence at the plate. I highly recommend this for anyone who is struggling trying to hit the ball. 5 Stars For Sure!
Love this app and training tool but it sometimes hangs during launch which can be frustrating.

This has been a huge help for my son's pitch recognition and timing of off speed pitches. He looks forward to getting his reps in now and I don't have to take him to a batting cage or throw out my arm doing it. It is just wonderful!
I have seen outstanding results for both of my boys from using this. It’s great during the winter months when it’s to cold to get BP in outside.

reviews.review_title: Quality Reps in the Middle of Winter!
reviews.review_description: My boys got this for Christmas and living in a state with cold winters, this makes it easy to get work in from home. We like that the dashboard tracks hits making it helpful for their 5,000 swings challenge.
Great for seeing pitches and working on timing. Once you figure out the functionality of the app it is a breeze after. Recommended

Love the game and I really improved my hitting skills by playing it. My coaches told me that they really say improvement in my swing, and it’s a great way to practice when it’s to hit to go outside. Would recommend!
